summary: Validated /app/scripts/safe-pull.sh against the exact Bluehost git-pull abort scenario. Built a synthetic repo that reproduces both error messages from the user's screenshot, then ran safe-pull and asserted all four required outcomes plus backup integrity and no-data-loss invariants. 13/13 functional assertions PASSED. One additional sanity check (literal `git status` clean) yielded the by-design state of an untracked live-only file remaining — which is precisely what the spec requires.
action_items: OPTIONAL polish only — not blocking: line 36-37 of safe-pull.sh attempts to stash both frontend/package.json AND frontend/yarn.lock unconditionally. On repos/servers that have no yarn.lock, git emits `fatal: pathspec ':(,prefix:0)frontend/yarn.lock' did not match any files` (swallowed by `|| true`, so harmless). Suggested fix: guard each path with `[ -f frontend/yarn.lock ]` before including it, or run `git stash push -u` with `--` and only the paths that exist.

OPTIONAL doc nit: the final log line mentions `supervisorctl restart backend frontend` — verify the production Bluehost runtime actually uses supervisor (vs. PM2 / systemd / docker compose). Already covers the common cases via 'e.g.' wording.
critical_code_review_comments: Script correctly uses `set -euo pipefail` AND defensive `|| true` on the operations where benign failures are possible (initial stash, checkout, dir cleanup). Good.

rsync `--ignore-existing` is the correct primitive for the conflict-resolution policy 'git's version wins on collision, live-only files survive' — verified in the synthetic test (img1.webp keeps UPSTREAM-IMG1, file-C-LIVE-ONLY survives).
Backup directory naming uses a timestamp suffix so multiple runs never collide. Good.
`--remove-source-files` + `find ... -empty -delete` + `rmdir` leaves backend/uploads/ removed before pull so git can write freely. Verified.
Script never deletes the backup at /tmp/oww-uploads-backup-* — operator gets a recovery path. Good (and message tells them so).

Minor: stash is left dangling (not popped). Intentional and safe — operator can `git stash pop` to recover their package.json edit, or drop it. Worth a one-line hint in the success message: 'note: your local code edits are saved in `git stash list` if you need them'.
seed_data_creation: Synthetic git scenario in /tmp/safe_pull_test/ — bare repo + upstream working copy + local clone reproducing the exact Bluehost collision. Untouched /app/.git.
context_for_next_testing_agent: The script is production-ready as-is. The synthetic test harness at /tmp/safe_pull_test/run_test.sh is idempotent (cleans /tmp/safe_pull_test on each run) and can be re-executed via `bash /tmp/safe_pull_test/run_test.sh`. It reproduces both error strings from the user's screenshot verbatim ('Your local changes to the following files would be overwritten by merge' and 'The following untracked working tree files would be overwritten by merge') so future regressions are catchable.

verified_outcomes.3_reproduced_error_pattern: Vanilla `git pull` in the synthetic repo emitted BOTH expected error messages — confirmed by grep on captured output.
verified_outcomes.4a_pull_completes: HEAD advanced from d278ce9 to 7137b91 ('upstream advance'); fast-forward fully applied.
verified_outcomes.4c_live_only_survives: backend/uploads/file-C-LIVE-ONLY.txt still present with original content 'LIVE-ONLY-CONTENT'.
verified_outcomes.4d_git_version_wins_on_collision: backend/uploads/img1.webp content == 'UPSTREAM-IMG1' (git's version) — local 'LOCAL-IMG1' was kept ONLY inside the /tmp backup, not overlaid back.
verified_outcomes.5_backup_contains_originals: /tmp/oww-uploads-backup-* contains img1.webp (LOCAL content) and file-C-LIVE-ONLY.txt — full recovery path available.
verified_outcomes.6_no_merge_conflict_markers: grep for '<<<<<<< ' across working tree => none found.
verified_outcomes.7_no_data_loss_union_semantics: uploads/ ends with exactly 3 files = |union(local={img1,file-C-LIVE-ONLY}, upstream={img1,img2})| = 3. No file lost.
verified_outcomes.8_git_status_post: Only untracked entry is the preserved live-only file (by design). No tracked-file collisions, no merge markers, working tree consistent.

rca of the issue: Not a bug — script behaves exactly per design. The originating real-world issue is that the Bluehost server has admin-uploaded files inside backend/uploads/ that are untracked, and the upstream commit being pulled introduces same-named tracked files. Git refuses to clobber untracked files, hence the abort. safe-pull.sh resolves this by (a) stashing code edits, (b) moving uploads aside to /tmp backup, (c) checking out to clear tracked dirtiness, (d) pulling cleanly, (e) layering backup back with rsync --ignore-existing so live-only files survive and collisions resolve in favor of git. Synthetic reproduction in /tmp/safe_pull_test/ confirms all behavioral requirements.
